t6_4_hardening_con_nyx_post_edit.scope: Third T6.4 hardening pass addressing con-nyx post-edit review of commit 96fd850 (which was timed just before commit 6b4455f; 6b4455f already covered con-nyx's hurdle-bounds portion of finding (j)). This commit closes the remaining CONDITIONAL (c) hours=None D6-bypass + cheap NICEs (b) docstring + (d) flag-OFF parity antibody + (h) local-import cleanup + (i) fee_rate two-source-of-truth coupling.
changes: CONDITIONAL (c) hours=None authority-gap breadcrumb: when flag ON and hours_to_settlement is None or <0, _buy_yes_exit + _buy_no_exit now append 'hold_value_hours_unknown_time_cost_zero' to applied_validations. Surfaces the D6-bypass via monitor summary counts instead of silent degradation. 3 new tests lock the behavior at both buy_yes/buy_no paths + regression guard that breadcrumb does NOT fire when hours is valid.

NICE (b) capital_locked semantic docstring: hold_value.py compute_with_exit_costs docstring clarified — mark-to-market (shares × best_bid) is D6-correct over entry notional (size_usd) because entry is sunk cost; what's actually locked is the exit value you'd release by closing now.

NICE (d) flag-OFF parity antibody: test_flag_off_parity_with_pre_t64_bare_math exercises _buy_yes_exit with flag OFF and asserts the decision matches the pre-T6.4 bare-math `sell_value <= hold_gross` formula inline. Belt-and-suspenders against silent regression in flag-OFF preservation path.

NICE (h) local-import cleanup: polymarket_fee import moved to module-level in hold_value.py. Verified no circular dependency (execution_price does not import hold_value). Cleaner, eliminates per-call import-cache lookup.

NICE (i) fee_rate coupling antibody: test_fee_rate_config_matches_polymarket_fee_default pins config.exit.fee_rate == polymarket_fee.__defaults__ at CI time. Drift in either source is caught before deploy.

t6_4_phase2_closed.scope_delivered: D6 correlation crowding cost wired through ExitContext. ExitContext dataclass gains portfolio_positions: tuple = () + bankroll: Optional[float] = None (both trailing defaults, frozen-dataclass safe). cycle_runtime._build_exit_context() accepts portfolio kwarg and populates the tuple from PortfolioState.positions (self-excluded via trade_id filter). _buy_yes_exit / _buy_no_exit signatures extended with portfolio_positions + bankroll kwargs; 4 evaluate_exit call sites thread them through. Helper _compute_exit_correlation_crowding(this_cluster, portfolio_positions, bankroll, shares, best_bid, crowding_rate) reuses src/strategy/correlation.py::get_correlation and returns dollar-denominated crowding cost: rate × exposure_ratio × shares × best_bid. Cost plumbed into all 4 HoldValue.compute_with_exit_costs factory calls via correlation_crowding kwarg; applied_validations breadcrumb `hold_value_correlation_crowding_applied` fires when cost > 0. Config: exit.correlation_crowding_rate default 0.0 (safe no-op — phase2 shipping does NOT alter live behavior until operator flips after T6.3-followup-1 replay audit), bounded [0, 0.1] via getter.
t6_4_phase2_closed.actual_size_estimate: ~4h (reused correlated_exposure semantics via direct get_correlation loop; no new invariants introduced at ExitContext authority layer — portfolio_positions + bankroll are optional trailing defaults)
t6_4_phase2_closed.category_immunity_delta: D6 0.7/1.0 → 1.0/1.0 when exit.correlation_crowding_rate > 0. The architectural seam is now complete; crowding activation becomes a config flip rather than code change. Silent D6 bypass at the correlation dimension is now structurally impossible (any non-zero rate flows through typed ExitContext → helper → HoldValue factory with breadcrumb).
